description:
## Background The four Issue List WebDriver specs each redeclare the same typed-RPC interfaces, `executeAsync` lambdas, and DOM helpers, and each inline the same sidebar selectors. This is two smells (Fowler, *Refactoring*) with one shared fix: - **Duplicated Code** — the same interfaces, lambdas, and helpers are copy-pasted across specs with subtle drift (`arguments_: object` typed signature is present in `issue-list.empty.spec.ts` and `issue-list.restoration.spec.ts` but absent from `issue-list.atomic-switch.spec.ts`). - **Shotgun Surgery** — one sidebar markup change (the recent `p.text-muted` → `p.truncate` swap) forced a synchronized edit in four specs at once. Per-file redeclarations: - `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.atomic-switch.spec.ts:40-180` — `WorkspaceSwitchResponse`, `WorkspaceStateResponse`, `invokeTypedWorkspaceSwitch`, `invokeWorkspaceState`, `issueRowSelector`, `expectIssueVisible`, `expectSidebarWorkspacePath`. - `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.success.spec.ts:32-160` — the same five symbols (plus an extra top-level `state` wrapper). - `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.empty.spec.ts` and `e2e/issue-list/issue-list.restoration.spec.ts` — the same surfaces again, with subtle signature drift on the typed-transport `executeAsync` lambda. The sidebar selector literal `"[aria-label='Workspace'] p.truncate"` also appears inline in every spec. This epic merges the previous `bsm-kia.13` (Duplicated Code) and `bsm-kia.14` (Shotgun Surgery) plans because the helpers module addresses both smells together — extracting helpers without isolating selectors would leave shotgun surgery, and isolating selectors without extracting helpers would leave duplication. ## What to build Two sibling helper modules under `e2e/issue-list/helpers/` that own the canonical implementations of every redeclared surface and every sidebar selector literal. The four specs import named helpers only; nothing in `e2e/issue-list/*.spec.ts` redeclares any of the above types, lambdas, helpers, or sidebar selectors. - `e2e/issue-list/helpers/rpc.ts` — typed-RPC interfaces, the `executeAsync` lambdas, and the row/visibility DOM helpers. - `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts` — sidebar selector strings and named assertions (`expectCurrentWorkspace`, `expectNoCurrentWorkspace`, `expectRememberedWorkspace`). ## Technical Approach This is a **wide refactor** — its blast radius fans across the four spec files. Sequence as expand–contract per the project template: 1. **Expand** (`bsm-kia.13.1`) — create both helper modules side by side, fully populated, with no spec importing them yet. Two new files in one commit; existing local copies remain. 2. **Migrate** (`bsm-kia.13.2`) — replace every local declaration of the affected interfaces, lambdas, helpers, and inline sidebar selectors in all four specs with imports from the helper modules. One commit per directory; behavior stays byte-for-byte equivalent; existing local copies still exist as dead code (not yet deleted). 3. **Contract** (`bsm-kia.13.3`) — delete the local copies and inline selectors in every spec. Verify `rg "interface WorkspaceSwitchResponse|interface WorkspaceStateResponse|invokeTypedWorkspaceSwitch|invokeWorkspaceState|issueRowSelector|expectIssueVisible|expectSidebarWorkspacePath|expectSidebarWorkspaceAbsent|\[aria-label='Workspace'\]|p\.truncate|p\.text-muted"` returns zero hits in `e2e/issue-list/*.spec.ts`. Update `docs/agents/webdriver-e2e.md` if it referenced the old per-spec helpers or inline selectors. ## Considerations - Land both expands (`helpers/rpc.ts` and `helpers/sidebar.ts`) in the same commit; they ship together because every spec that imports one will import both. - The migrate batch is per-directory (`e2e/issue-list/`): all four specs switch in one commit. The full E2E suite (`pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete`) must pass before the contract step. - Keep one canonical `arguments_: object` typed signature across all callers — no per-spec drift. - The helper signatures must match the generated TauRPC binding types in `src/rpc/bindings.ts`. - The selector inside `helpers/sidebar.ts` is the single source of truth for the sidebar selector; future `WorkspaceSelector.tsx` rename or restructure touches one helper file, not four. ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] `e2e/issue-list/helpers/rpc.ts` and `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts` exist and export the canonical types, lambdas, helpers, and sidebar selectors. - [ ] Each spec imports from the helper modules; no spec redeclares the affected interfaces, lambdas, helpers, or inline sidebar selectors. - [ ] `rg "interface WorkspaceSwitchResponse|interface WorkspaceStateResponse|invokeTypedWorkspaceSwitch|invokeWorkspaceState|issueRowSelector|expectIssueVisible|expectSidebarWorkspacePath|expectSidebarWorkspaceAbsent|\[aria-label='Workspace'\]|p\.truncate|p\.text-muted"` returns zero hits in `e2e/issue-list/*.spec.ts`. - [ ] All four specs continue to assert everything they assert today (verified by `pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete` and `pnpm e2e:issue-list:isolation` passing). - [ ] No spec-local type drift between `arguments_: object` and `Record`. - [ ] Renaming the sidebar `

` class in `WorkspaceSelector.tsx` now touches one helper file, not four spec files. ## Test Plan - Vitest unit tests for any pure helpers in either module (e.g., `issueRowSelector` formatting, sidebar basename matching). - Run `pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete` and `pnpm e2e:issue-list:isolation` after the migrate batch and after the contract to prove no behavioral regression at either boundary. ## Sub-issues - `bsm-kia.13.1` — Expand: create `helpers/rpc.ts` and `helpers/sidebar.ts` - `bsm-kia.13.2` — Migrate all four specs to the helpers - `bsm-kia.13.3` — Contract: delete local copies and inline selectors, verify `rg`
title: Collapse duplicate Workspace RPC and sidebar helpers across the four e2e specs
